name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
وَٱتَّبَعُوا۟ مَا تَتْلُوا۟ ٱلشَّيَـٰطِينُ عَلَىٰ مُلْكِ سُلَيْمَـٰنَ ۖ وَمَا كَفَرَ سُلَيْمَـٰنُ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱلشَّيَـٰطِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ يُعَلِّمُونَ ٱلنَّاسَ ٱلسِّحْرَ وَمَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَى ٱلْمَلَكَيْنِ بِبَابِلَ هَـٰرُوتَ وَمَـٰرُوتَ ۚ وَمَا يُعَلِّمَانِ مِنْ أَحَدٍ حَتَّىٰ يَقُولَآ إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ فِتْنَةٌ فَلَا تَكْفُرْ ۖ فَيَتَعَلَّمُونَ مِنْهُمَا مَا يُفَرِّقُونَ بِهِۦ بَيْنَ ٱلْمَرْءِ وَزَوْجِهِۦ ۚ وَمَا هُم بِضَآرِّينَ بِهِۦ مِنْ أَحَدٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَيَتَعَلَّمُونَ مَا يَضُرُّهُمْ وَلَا يَنفَعُهُمْ ۚ وَلَقَدْ عَلِمُوا۟ لَمَنِ ٱشْتَرَىٰهُ مَا لَهُۥ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ مِنْ خَلَـٰقٍ ۚ وَلَبِئْسَ مَا شَرَوْا۟ بِهِۦٓ أَنفُسَهُمْ ۚ لَوْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْلَمُونَ (١٠٢)
```

```english
And they followed what the devils pursued during Solomon’s reign —and Solomon was not faithless but it was the devils who were faithless—teaching the people magic and what was sent down to the two angels at Babylon, Harut and Marut, who would not teach anyone without telling [him], ‘We are only a test, so do not be faithless.’ But they would learn from those two that with which they would cause a split between man and his wife—though they could not harm anyone with it except with Allah’s leave. They would learn that which would harm them and bring them no benefit; though they certainly knew that anyone who buys it has no share in the Hereafter. Surely, evil is that for which they sold their souls, had they known! (102)
```

```arabic
وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَٱتَّقَوْا۟ لَمَثُوبَةٌ مِّنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ خَيْرٌ ۖ لَّوْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْلَمُونَ (١٠٣)
```

```english
Had they been faithful and Godwary, the reward from Allah would have been better, had they known! (103)
```

## (Commentary)

**His saying, the Exalted:** "And they followed what the devils pursued during the reign of Solomon..." to the end of the verse. The commentators have differed in the interpretation of this verse with a strange divergence, the like of which is hardly found in any other verse of the Majestic Quran. They differed regarding the referent of the pronoun in His saying: "And they followed" (واتبعوا) — *the act of adopting and adhering to a path or practice*. Are they the Jews who lived during the time of Solomon, or those during the time of the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله‌وسلم), or all of them? They differed regarding His saying: "pursued" (تتلوا) — *to follow, recite, or fabricate*. Is it in the sense that the devils followed it and acted upon it, or in the sense of reciting, or in the sense of lying? They differed regarding His saying: "the devils" (الشياطين). It was said they are the devils of the jinn (الجن), and it was said they are the devils of men (الإنس), and it was said they are both together. They differed regarding His saying: "during the reign of Solomon" (على ملك سليمان). It was said it means "in" the reign of Solomon, and it was said it means "during the era" of Solomon's reign, and it was said it means "against" the reign of Solomon—preserving the literal sense of superiority in the meaning of "upon" (على) — and it was said it means "upon the era" of Solomon's reign. They differed regarding His saying: "but the devils disbelieved" (ولكن الشياطين كفروا). It was said they disbelieved by what they extracted of sorcery (السحر) — *the use of hidden forces to produce supernatural effects* — to the people; it was said they disbelieved by what they attributed to Solomon of sorcery; and it was said they practiced sorcery, so sorcery was expressed as disbelief (الكفر). They differed regarding His saying: "teaching the people sorcery" (يعلمون الناس السحر). It was said they cast sorcery to them and they learned it; it was said they directed the people to the extraction of sorcery which was buried under Solomon's throne, so they extracted it and learned it. They differed regarding His saying: "and what was sent down to the two angels" (وما أنزل على الملكين). It was said that "what" (ما) is a relative pronoun and the conjunction is to His saying: "what they pursued" (ما تتلوه); it was said "what" is a relative pronoun and the conjunction is to His saying: "sorcery" (السحر), meaning they teach them what was sent down to the two angels; and it was said "what" is a negating particle and the "and" (الواو) is for commencement, meaning: "and sorcery was not sent down to the two angels as the Jews claim." They differed regarding the meaning of "sending down" (الإنزال). It was said it was a sending down from the heavens, and it was said rather from the highlands and elevations of the earth. They differed regarding His saying: "the two angels" (الملكين). It was said they were two of the angels of heaven; it was said rather they were two human kings (مَلِكَيْن) with a *kasrah* on the *lam* if we read it as such, as it was read in the irregular recitations; or two angels (مَلَكَيْن) with a *fathah* on the *lam*, meaning two righteous men, or two who feigned righteousness, if we read it according to the well-known recitation. They differed regarding His saying: "at Babylon" (ببابل). It was said it is Babylon of Iraq; it was said Babylon of Damavand; and it was said from Nasibin to Ra's al-Ayn. They differed regarding His saying: "and they did not teach" (وما يعلمان). It was said "taught" (علّم) in its literal sense, and it was said "taught" in the sense of "informed." They differed regarding His saying: "so do not disbelieve" (فلا تكفر). It was said: "do not disbelieve by practicing sorcery"; it was said: "do not disbelieve by learning it"; and it was said by both together.

They differed regarding His saying: "so they learn from them" (فيتعلمون منهما). It was said: meaning from Harut and Marut; it was said: meaning from sorcery and disbelief; and it was said: as a substitute for what the two angels taught by way of prohibition against doing it. They differed regarding His saying: "that by which they cause a separation between a man and his wife" (ما يفرقون به بين المرء وزوجه). It was said: meaning they create love and hatred between them; it was said they entice one of the spouses and incite them toward disbelief and polytheism (الشرك), so the difference in creed and religion separates them; and it was said they strive between them with slander and malicious gossip, which leads to separation. This is a portion of the differences in the interpretation of the words and sentences of the verse containing the story. There are other differences outside the story in the conclusion of the verse and in the story itself: is it a factual story or an explanation by way of parable? Or something else? If you multiply some of the numbers of possibilities we mentioned by others, the possibilities rise to an astonishing amount, nearly one million two hundred and sixty thousand possibilities (4 x 39 x 24)!

By the life of Allah, this is among the wonders of the Quran's composition (نظم) — *the precise arrangement and structural harmony of the text*. The verse fluctuates between doctrines and possibilities that baffle the intellects and bewilder the minds, yet the speech remains reclining upon the throne of its beauty, adorned in its most exquisite splendor, embellished with the ornaments of its eloquence (البلاغة) and lucidity (الفصاحة). A similar instance to this verse will come before you in His saying, the Exalted: **أَفَمَن كَانَ عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّهِ وَيَتْلُوهُ شَاهِدٌ مِّنْهُ وَمِن قَبْلِهِ كِتَابُ مُوسَىٰ إِمَامًا وَرَحْمَةً** "Is he who stands on a manifest proof from his Lord, and whom a witness from Him follows, and before him was the Book of Moses, a guide and a mercy..." (Hud: 17).

What ought to be said is that the verse, by its context, addresses another affair among the affairs of the Jews, which is the circulation of sorcery among them, and that they used to base its origin on a well-known story or two stories known to them, in which there is mention of the matter of Solomon the Prophet and the two angels at Babylon, Harut and Marut. Thus, the speech is conjoined to the story they possess and claim. However, the Jews—as the Quran mentions of them—are a people of distortion (تحريف) and alteration (تغيير) regarding knowledge and truths. One cannot trust, nor is it safe from their conduct, that they would present historical stories without distorting and altering them, as is their habit with knowledge; they incline at every moment toward what suits their interests in word and deed. What appears from the folds of the verse's sentences is sufficient. In any case, it appears from the verse that the Jews were practicing sorcery among themselves, attributing it to Solomon, claiming that Solomon (عليه‌السلام) only possessed the kingdom and subjugated the jinn, humans, wild beasts, and birds, and performed wondrous and miraculous feats through sorcery, which is part of what was in their hands. They attributed another part of it to the two angels at Babylon, Harut and Marut. So the Quran refuted them, stating that Solomon (عليه‌السلام) did not practice sorcery. How could he, when sorcery is disbelief in Allah and an interference in the universe contrary to the habit Allah established, manifesting it upon the imagination and senses of living beings? Solomon (عليه‌السلام) did not disbelieve, for he was an infallible prophet (نبي معصوم). This is His saying, the Exalted: **وَمَا كَفَرَ سُلَيْمَانُ وَلَـٰكِنَّ الشَّيَاطِينَ كَفَرُوا يُعَلِّمُونَ النَّاسَ السِّحْرَ** "Solomon did not faithlessly contend, but it was the devils who were faithless, teaching the people magic..." and His saying, the Exalted: **وَلَقَدْ عَلِمُوا لَمَنِ اشْتَرَاهُ مَا لَهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ مِنْ خَلَاقٍ** "...And they certainly knew that whoever buys it has no share in the Hereafter." Solomon (عليه‌السلام) is of a higher standing and more sacred presence than to have sorcery and disbelief attributed to him. Allah has magnified his status in several places of His speech in various Meccan surahs revealed before this surah, such as Surah al-An'am, al-Anbiya, al-Naml, and Surah Sad. In these, it is mentioned that he was a righteous servant and a sent prophet to whom Allah gave knowledge and wisdom, and bestowed upon him a kingdom that would not belong to anyone after him. Thus, he was not a sorcerer; rather, these are superstitious stories and myths (الأساطير) which the devils fabricated and "pursued" (تلوها) — recited — to their allies among men, and they disbelieved by misleading the people through the teaching of sorcery. The Quran also refuted them regarding the two angels at Babylon, Harut and Marut, stating that even if that was sent down to them, there is no harm in that, for it was a trial (فتنة) and a divine test (امتحان), just as He inspired the hearts of the children of Adam with the ways of evil and corruption as a trial and test, and it is part of the Decree (القدر). So, although sorcery was sent down to them, they would not teach anyone without saying to him: "We are only a trial, so do not disbelieve" by using the sorcery you learn in an improper place, such as for nullifying sorcery or exposing the transgression of its practitioners. Yet, they would learn from them that by which they corrupt the most righteous thing Allah placed in nature and habit, separating thereby between a man and his wife, seeking evil and corruption, and learning what harms them and does not benefit them.

Thus, His saying, the Exalted: "And they followed" (واتبعوا) means the Jews who came after the era of Solomon followed, through inheritance from predecessors to successors, "what they pursued" (ما تتلوا), meaning what the devils among the jinn fabricated and lied about "during the reign of Solomon." The evidence that "pursued" (تتلوا) is in the sense of "lied" is its being made transitive with the preposition "against" (على). The evidence that the devils are the jinn is that these were under the subjugation of Solomon and punished by his punishment; by that, he (عليه‌السلام) would restrain them from corruption. The Exalted said: **وَمِنَ الشَّيَاطِينِ مَن يَغُوصُونَ لَهُ وَيَعْمَلُونَ عَمَلًا دُونَ ذَٰلِكَ وَكُنَّا لَهُمْ حَافِظِينَ** "And of the devils were some who dived for him and performed other tasks besides that, and We were watchful over them" (al-Anbiya: 82). And the Exalted said: **فَلَمَّا خَرَّ تَبَيَّنَتِ الْجِنُّ أَن لَّوْ كَانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ الْغَيْبَ مَا لَبِثُوا فِي الْعَذَابِ الْمُهِينِ** "...when he fell down, the jinn realized that had they known the Unseen, they would not have remained in a humiliating punishment" (Saba: 14).

**His saying, the Exalted:** "Solomon did not disbelieve," meaning while the situation was that Solomon did not practice sorcery such that he would disbelieve, "but the devils disbelieved," while the situation was that they were misleading the people and teaching them sorcery.

**His saying, the Exalted:** "And what was sent down," meaning the Jews followed what was sent down by way of notification and inspiration (الإلهام) — *the divine casting of an idea into the heart* — to the two angels at Babylon, Harut and Marut, while the situation was that they would not teach sorcery to anyone until they warned him against practicing it, saying: "We are only a trial for you and a test by which you are tested through what we teach you, so do not disbelieve by using it."

**His saying, the Exalted:** "So they learn from them," meaning from the two angels, Harut and Marut, "that by which they cause a separation," meaning a sorcery by whose practice and effect they separate between a man and his wife.

**His saying, the Exalted:** "But they do not harm anyone with it except by Allah's permission." This is a rebuttal to what might occur to the imagination (الوهم) — *the faculty that perceives particular meanings* — that they, by that, corrupt the order of creation and existence (التكوين), outstrip the decree of Allah, and nullify His command. He refuted this by stating that sorcery itself is part of the Decree (القدر); it does not affect except by Allah's permission, so they cannot frustrate Him. He placed this sentence before His saying: "And they learn what harms them and does not benefit them," because this sentence—meaning: "So they learn from them that by which they cause a separation between a man and his wife"—alone contains the mention of the effect, so it was followed by the statement that this effect is by Allah's permission.

**His saying, the Exalted:** "And they certainly knew that whoever buys it has no share in the Hereafter." They knew that by their intellects (العقول), for the intellect does not doubt that sorcery is the most ominous source of corruption in human society. They also knew that from the words of Moses, for he is the one who said: **وَلَا يُفْلِحُ السَّاحِرُ حَيْثُ أَتَىٰ** "...and the magician does not succeed wherever he may come" (Taha: 69).

**His saying, the Exalted:** "Surely evil is what they sold themselves for, had they known," meaning that despite their knowing it to be an evil for them and a corrupter of their Hereafter, they were as if they did not know it, since they did not act upon what they knew. For knowledge (العلم), if it does not guide its possessor to the straight path (الصراط المستقيم), is misguidance and ignorance, not knowledge. The Exalted said: **أَفَرَأَيْتَ مَنِ اتَّخَذَ إِلَٰهَهُ هَوَاهُ وَأَضَلَّهُ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ عِلْمٍ** "Have you seen him who has taken his desire to be his god and whom Allah has led astray knowingly..." (al-Jathiyah: 23). Thus, these people, despite their knowledge of the matter, are such that one should wish for them knowledge and guidance.

**His saying, the Exalted:** "And had they believed and been God-conscious," etc., meaning had they followed faith (الإيمان) and godwariness (التقوى) — *the state of being conscious of Allah's presence and avoiding His displeasure* — instead of following the myths of the devils and the disbelief of sorcery. In this is evidence that disbelief through sorcery is disbelief at the level of action (العمل), like the abandonment of zakat (الزكاة), not disbelief at the level of belief (الاعتقاد). Had sorcery been disbelief in belief, the Exalted would have said: "And had they believed, a reward..." etc., and restricted it to faith without mentioning godwariness. The Jews believed, but since they were not godwary and did not observe the prohibitions of Allah, He did not give weight to their faith, and they were as disbelievers.

**His saying, the Exalted:** "a reward from Allah would have been better, had they known," meaning better than the rewards and benefits they seek through sorcery and acquire through disbelief.

## (Narrative Discussion)

In the *Tafsir* of al-Ayyashi and al-Qummi, regarding His saying, the Exalted: "And they followed what the devils pursued during the reign of Solomon," narrated from Imam al-Baqir (عليه‌السلام) in a tradition: **فلما هلك سليمان وضع إبليس السحر وكتبه في كتاب ثم طواه وكتب على ظهره ، هذا ما وضع آصف بن برخيا للملك سليمان بن داود من ذخائر كنوز العلم من أراد كذا وكذا فليعمل كذا وكذا ثم دفنه تحت سريره ثم استتاره لهم فقرأه فقال الكافرون : ما كان يغلبنا سليمان إلا بهذا ، وقال المؤمنون : بل هو عبد الله ونبيه ، فقال الله جل ذكره : وإتبعوا ما تتلوا الشياطين على ملك سليمان** — "When Solomon died, Iblis fabricated sorcery and wrote it in a book, then rolled it up and wrote on its back: 'This is what Asaf ibn Barkhiya placed for King Solomon son of David of the treasures of the hoards of knowledge; whoever wants such-and-such, let him do such-and-such.' Then he buried it under his throne, then uncovered it for them and read it. The disbelievers said: 'Solomon only used to overcome us with this.' The believers said: 'Rather, he is the servant of Allah and His prophet.' Then Allah, Majestic is His mention, said: 'And they followed what the devils pursued during the reign of Solomon.'"

**I say:** Attributing the fabrication, writing, and reading to Iblis does not contradict its attribution to the rest of the devils among the jinn and men, for all evil terminates in him and spreads from him—may Allah curse him—to his allies through inspiration (الوحي) and whispering (الوسوسة). This is common in the language of the narrations (الأخبار). The literal sense of the tradition is that the word "pursued" (تتلوه) is from "recitation" (التلاوة) in the sense of reading. This does not contradict what we deemed apparent in the previous commentary: that "pursued" (تتلو) is in the sense of "lied," because the implication of the meaning of lying is through inclusion (تضمين) or something similar. The estimation of His saying: "the devils pursued during the reign of Solomon" is: "they read it, lying against the reign of Solomon." The root of the meaning of "pursued" (تلا يتلو) returns to the meaning of "followed" (ولى يلى) and "guardianship" (الولاية), which is to possess a thing in terms of order and the occurrence of one part of it after another. The discussion on this will come in Surah al-Ma'idah in the commentary on His saying, the Exalted: **إِنَّمَا وَلِيُّكُمُ اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ** "Your guardian is only Allah, His Apostle..." (al-Ma'idah: 55).

In *al-Uyun*, in a tradition of Imam al-Rida (عليه‌السلام) with al-Ma'mun: **وأما هاروت وماروت فكانا ملكين علما الناس السحر ليتحرزوا به عن سحر السحرة ويبطلوا كيدهم وما علما أحداً من ذلك شيئاً إلا قالا له إنما نحن فتنة فلا تكفر فكفر قوم باستعمالهم لما امروا بالاحتراز عنه وجعلوا يفرقون بما يعملونه بين المرء وزوجه ، قال الله تعالى : وما هم بضارين به من أحد إلا بإذن الله** — "As for Harut and Marut, they were two angels who taught the people sorcery so they might guard themselves thereby against the sorcery of the sorcerers and nullify their plot. They did not teach anyone anything of that except that they said to him: 'We are only a trial, so do not disbelieve.' But a people disbelieved by their use of what they were commanded to guard against, and they began to separate, by what they practiced, between a man and his wife. Allah the Exalted said: 'But they do not harm anyone with it except by Allah's permission.'"

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, Ibn Jarir narrated from Ibn Abbas, who said: **كان سليمان إذا أراد أن يدخل الخلاء أو يأتي شيئاً من شأنه أعطى الجرادة وهي إمرأته خاتمه فلما أراد الله أن يبتلى سليمان بالذي ابتلاه به أعطى الجرادة ذلك اليوم خاتمه فجاء الشيطان في صورة سليمان فقال لها : هاتي خاتمي فأخذه ولبسه فلما لبسه دانت له شياطين الجن والإنس فجائها سليمان فقال : هاتي خاتمي فقالت كذبت لست سليمان فعرف أنه بلاء ابتلى به فانطلقت الشياطين فكتبت في تلك الأيام كتباً فيها سحر وكفر ثم دفنوها تحت كرسي سليمان ثم أخرجوها فقرئوها على الناس فقالوا إنما كان سليمان يغلب الناس بهذه الكتب فبرء الناس من سليمان واكفروه حتى بعث الله محمداً وأنزل عليه : وما كفر سليمان ولكن الشياطين كفروا** — "Whenever Solomon wanted to enter the privy or attend to some of his affairs, he would give al-Jaradah—who was his wife—his ring. When Allah willed to test Solomon with that which He tested him, he gave al-Jaradah his ring that day. Then the devil came in the form of Solomon and said to her: 'Give me my ring.' He took it and wore it. When he wore it, the devils of the jinn and men became subservient to him. Then Solomon came to her and said: 'Give me my ring.' She said: 'You have lied, you are not Solomon.' So he knew it was a trial he was tested with. The devils then went and wrote, during those days, books containing sorcery and disbelief, then buried them under Solomon's throne, then extracted them and read them to the people. They said: 'Solomon only used to overcome the people with these books.' So the people washed their hands of Solomon and deemed him a disbeliever until Allah sent Muhammad and revealed to him: 'Solomon did not disbelieve, but it was the devils who disbelieved.'"

**I say:** The story is narrated in other reports; it is a long story among the stories mentioned regarding the "slips of the prophets."

In *al-Durr al-Manthur* as well, Sa'id ibn Jarir and al-Khatib in his *Tarikh* narrated from Nafi', who said: **سافرت مع ابن عمر فلما كان في آخر الليل ، قال يا نافع : انظر هل طلعت الحمراء ؟ قلت : لا ، مرتين أو ثلثاً ثم قلت : قد طلعت . قال : لا مرحباً بها ولا أهلاً . قلت : سبحان الله نجم مسخر سامع مطيع . قال ما قلت لك إلا ما سمعت من رسول الله صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله‌وسلم . قال : إن الملائكة قالت : يا رب كيف صبرك على بني آدم في الخطايا والذنوب ؟ قال : إني أبليتهم وعافيتهم . قالوا لو كنا مكانهم ما عصيناك ، قال : فاختاروا ملكين منكم ، فلم يألوا جهداً أن يختاروا فاختاروا هاروت وماروت فنزلا ، فألقى الله عليهما الشبق . قلت : وما الشبق ؟ قال : الشهوة فجائت امرأة يقال لها الزهرة فوقعت في قلوبهما فجعل كل واحد منهما يخفى عن صاحبه ما في نفسه ثم قال أحدهما للآخر هل وقع في نفسك ما وقع في قلبي ؟ قال : نعم ، فطالباها لأنفسهما فقالت لا امكنكما حتى تعلماني الاسم الذي تعرجان به إلى السماء وتهبطان فأبيا ثم سئلاها أيضاً فأبت . ففعلا فلما استطيرت طمسها الله كوكباً وقطع أجنحتهما ثم سألا التوبة من ربهما فخيرهما فقال ان شئتما رددتكما إلى ما كنتما عليه ، فإذا كان يوم القيامة عذبتكما ، وان شئتما عذبتكما في الدنيا فإذا كان يوم القيامة رددتكما إلى ما كنتما عليه ، فقال أحدهما لصاحبه إن عذاب الدنيا ينقطع ويزول فاختارا عذاب الدنيا على عذاب الآخرة فأوحى الله اليهما أن ائتيا بابل فانطلقا إلى بابل فخسف بهما فهما منكوسان بين السماء والأرض معذبان إلى يوم القيامة** — "I traveled with Ibn Umar, and when it was the end of the night, he said: 'O Nafi', look, has the Red One (Venus) risen?' I said: 'No,' twice or thrice. Then I said: 'It has risen.' He said: 'No welcome to it nor greeting.' I said: 'Glory be to Allah! A subservient star, hearing and obeying.' He said: 'I have only told you what I heard from the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله‌وسلم).' He said: 'The angels said: "O Lord, how is Your patience with the children of Adam in their errors and sins?" He said: "I have tested them and granted them well-being." They said: "Had we been in their place, we would not have disobeyed You." He said: "Then choose two angels from among you." They spared no effort in choosing, and they chose Harut and Marut. They descended, and Allah cast upon them *al-shabaq*.' I asked: 'What is *al-shabaq*?' He said: 'Lust. Then a woman called al-Zuhara (Venus) came and fell into their hearts. Each of them began to hide from his companion what was in his soul. Then one said to the other: "Has there fallen into your soul what has fallen into my heart?" He said: "Yes." So they sought her for themselves. She said: "I will not enable you until you teach me the Name by which you ascend to heaven and descend." They refused. Then they asked her again and she refused. So they did. When she was flown up, Allah transformed her into a star and cut off their wings. Then they asked for repentance from their Lord, and He gave them a choice. He said: "If you wish, I will return you to what you were, and on the Day of Resurrection I will punish you; or if you wish, I will punish you in this world, and on the Day of Resurrection I will return you to what you were." One said to his companion: "The punishment of this world is interrupted and ceases." So they chose the punishment of this world over the punishment of the Hereafter. Then Allah revealed to them to go to Babylon. They went to Babylon and were swallowed by the earth; they are suspended upside down between heaven and earth, punished until the Day of Resurrection.'"

**I say:** Something close to this has been narrated in some Shi'a books as a *marfu'* (مرفوع) — *a tradition attributed to the Prophet or an Imam without a complete chain* — narration from Imam al-Baqir (عليه‌السلام). Al-Suyuti narrated more than twenty traditions regarding this meaning in the matter of Harut, Marut, and al-Zuhara; they declared the chains of some of them to be authentic. At the end of their chains are several companions such as Ibn Abbas, Ibn Mas'ud, Ali, Abu al-Darda, Umar, Aisha, and Ibn Umar. This is a mythical story attributed to the honored angels, whose sanctity and purity of existence from polytheism and disobedience—the gravest polytheism and the foulest disobedience: idol worship, murder, adultery, and wine drinking—the Quran has explicitly stated. It also attributes to the planet Venus (الزهرة) that it was an adulterous woman who was transformed—which is a mockery—while it is a pure celestial planet in its vanguard and creation, by which Allah the Exalted swore in His saying: **الْجَوَارِ الْكُنَّسِ** "the planets that run their course and set" (al-Takwir: 16). Moreover, astronomy has today revealed its identity and uncovered its element, quantity, quality, and other affairs.

Thus, this story, like the one before it mentioned in the previous narration, corresponds to what the Jews possess—as it is said—of the story of Harut and Marut, that mythical story which resembles the myths of the Greeks regarding the planets and stars.

From here, it becomes clear to the contemplative researcher that these traditions—like others narrated regarding the disparagement of the prophets and their slips—are not free from fabrications (الدس) that the Jews inserted into them. They reveal their precise infiltration and deep influence among the scholars of tradition (أصحاب الحديث) in the early period; they played with their narrations through all the fabrication and mixing they desired, and they were aided in that by another group of people.

However, Allah, exalted is His Name, has placed His Book in a divine safeguard from the whims of the whimsical among His enemies. Whenever a devil from among their devils attempts to steal a hearing, he is followed by a manifest flame. The Mightiest of speakers said: **إِنَّا نَحْنُ نَزَّلْنَا الذِّكْرَ وَإِنَّا لَهُ لَحَافِظُونَ** "Indeed We have sent down the Reminder, and indeed We will preserve it" (al-Hijr: 9). And He said: **وَإِنَّهُ لَكِتَابٌ عَزِيزٌ لَّا يَأْتِيهِ الْبَاطِلُ مِن بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَلَا مِنْ خَلْفِهِ تَنزِيلٌ مِّنْ حَكِيمٍ حَمِيدٍ** "and indeed it is an august Book: falsehood cannot approach it from before it nor from behind it, [it is] a gradual revelation from One All-wise, All-laudable" (Fussilat: 41-42). And He said: **وَنُنَزِّلُ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ مَا هُوَ شِفَاءٌ وَرَحْمَةٌ لِّلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَلَا يَزِيدُ الظَّالِمِينَ إِلَّا خَسَارًا** "We send down in the Quran that which is a cure and mercy for the believers, and it increases the wrongdoers only in loss" (al-Isra: 82).

He spoke generally and did not restrict it. Thus, there is no mixing or fabrication except that the Quran repels it and manifests the loss of its author by exposing his condition and reading the page of his history. The Messenger of Allah said in what both sects have narrated: "Whatever agrees with the Book of Allah, take it; and whatever contradicts it, leave it." Thus, he gave a universal criterion (ميزان) by which the transmitted knowledge from him and his successors is weighed. In short, through the Quran, falsehood is repelled from the presence of truth, then its vanity soon appears and it is put to death in living hearts just as it was put to death in external realities. The Exalted said: **بَلْ نَقْذِفُ بِالْحَقِّ عَلَى الْبَاطِلِ فَيَدْمَغُهُ** "Rather We hurl the truth against falsehood, and it knocks out its brain..." (al-Anbiya: 18). And the Exalted said: **وَيُرِيدُ اللَّهُ أَن يُحِقَّ الْحَقَّ بِكَلِمَاتِهِ** "...and Allah desires to confirm the truth by His words..." (al-Anfal: 7). And the Exalted said: **لِيُحِقَّ الْحَقَّ وَيُبْطِلَ الْبَاطِلَ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْمُجْرِمُونَ** "that He might confirm the truth and bring falsehood to naught, though the guilty should be averse" (al-Anfal: 8). There is no meaning to confirming the truth nor to bringing falsehood to naught except the manifestation of their respective attributes.

Some people, especially those of our era who are deeply immersed in materialist research and intimidated by modern Western civilization, have derived a bad conclusion from this aforementioned reality. They have taken to discarding all that the Sunnah (السنة) — *the prophetic tradition* — of the Messenger of Allah contains and what the collections of narrations comprise. They have followed the path of negligence (التفريط) in this, in contrast to the path of excess (الإفراط) followed by some traditionists (الأخباريين), scholars of tradition, the Haruriyyah, and others, who accept every narrated report regardless of its nature. Just as absolute acceptance is a denial of the criteria established in the religion to distinguish truth from falsehood and an attribution of vanity and idle talk to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله‌وسلم), so too is total rejection a denial of them, and an annulment and nullification of the Majestic Book, which falsehood cannot approach from before it nor from behind it. He, Majestic is His praise, says: **مَا آتَاكُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَمَا نَهَاكُمْ عَنْهُ فَانتَهُوا** "...Take whatever the Apostle gives you, and relinquish whatever he forbids you..." (al-Hashr: 7). And the Exalted said: **وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَا مِن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا لِيُطَاعَ بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ** "We did not send any apostle but to be obeyed by Allah’s leave..." (al-Nisa: 64). For if the word of the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله‌وسلم) had no authority (حجية), or if what is transmitted of his word (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله‌وسلم) to us—the community of those absent during his era or existing after his departure from the world—had no authority, not a single stone of the religion would remain standing. Relying on transmission and tradition is something humans encounter and accept in their social life with a necessity they are compelled to by self-evidence; their human primordial nature (الفطرة) guides them to it, and they cannot do without it. As for the occurrence of fabrication and mixing in transmitted religious knowledge, it is not an innovation unique to religion. How could it be, when the mill of society in all its directions and pillars turns upon the daily general and specific circulating reports, and the presence of lying, fabrication, and mixing in them is even greater, and the hands of major and minor politics play with them more? We, according to our human primordial nature, do not act upon the mere hearing of reports transmitted to us in the social assembly; rather, we present each one of them to the criterion we possess by which it can be weighed. If it agrees with it and confirms it, we accept it; if it contradicts it and denies it, we discard it; and if nothing of its matter becomes clear and its truth is not distinguished from its falsehood, nor its honesty from its lying, we suspend judgment (توقفنا) regarding it without acceptance or rejection, based on the caution (الإحتياط) we are naturally disposed toward regarding evils and harms.

All of this is on the condition of expertise (الخبرة) in the type of report transmitted to us. As for that in which a person has no expertise regarding the reports and their contents, the way of the rational people (العقلاء) in society is to refer to the people of expertise and to take what they see and judge therein.

This is what our primordial construction in human society is based upon. The religious criterion established to distinguish truth from falsehood, as well as honesty from lying, does not differ from that; rather, it is the very same thing. It is the presentation to the Book of Allah: if something becomes clear from it, it is taken; if it does not become clear due to an ambiguity (شبهة), then one halts at the ambiguity. Regarding this, there are mutawatir (متواترة) — *successive and widely transmitted* — reports from the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله‌وسلم) and the Imams from his household. All of this applies to matters other than jurisprudential issues (المسائل الفقهية); as for those, the reference in researching them is the science of the principles of jurisprudence (أصول الفقه).

## (Philosophical Discussion)

It is well known that supernatural acts (أفعال خارقة للعادة) occur through observation and transmission. Rarely is one of us found who has not witnessed something of supernatural acts or had something of that transmitted to him—little or much. However, precise research into many of them reveals their return to ordinary natural causes. Many of these acts are those by which their practitioners strengthen themselves through habituation and practice, such as eating poisons, carrying heavy weights, and walking on a rope stretched in the air, among others. Many of them rely on natural causes hidden from the people and unknown to them, such as one who enters fire and is not burned by it due to coating his body with talc, or one who writes a book with no visible script that only its owner can read—having written with a fluid that only appears when the book is exposed to fire—and so on. Many of them occur through rapid movements hidden from the senses due to their speed, so the sense only perceives that it occurred without a natural cause, like the supernatural feats performed by practitioners of sleight of hand (الشعبذة). All of these are based on ordinary causes hidden from our senses or beyond our power. However, some of these supernatural acts cannot be analyzed into ordinary natural causes, such as informing about some of the unseen (المغيبات), especially what occurs in the future, and acts of love, hatred, binding, loosening, hypnotism, causing illness, sleep-binding, summoning, and movements by will (الإرادة) — *the faculty of choice and volition* — that occur from practitioners of spiritual disciplines (الرياضات). These are matters that cannot be denied; we have witnessed some of them and others have been transmitted to us through reports that cannot be impugned. Indeed, there exists today among their practitioners in India, Iran, and the West a group from whom types of these supernatural acts are witnessed. Complete reflection on the methods of spiritual disciplines that grant these supernatural acts, and practical experience in their actions and will, necessitates the statement that they are based on the power of the will and the belief in its effect, despite the diversity of their types. For the will follows knowledge and the conviction (الاذعان) preceding it. Sometimes it exists in its absolute form, and sometimes it exists upon the presence of specific conditions, such as writing something specific with a specific ink in a specific place in some acts of love and hatred, or placing a mirror facing the face of a specific child during the summoning of a spirit, or reciting a specific incantation (عوذة), and so on. All of these are conditions for the attainment of the acting will. Thus, when knowledge becomes complete and certain knowledge (علماً قاطعاً), it grants the senses the observation of what was certain. You can test the truth of that by suggesting to yourself that such-and-such a thing or person is present with you, whom you witness with your senses, then imagining it such that you do not doubt it and do not pay attention to its absence nor to anything else; you will find it before you as you desire. Sometimes there is found in the records the treatment by some physicians of fatal diseases by suggesting health to the patient.

If the matter is as such, then if the will becomes strong, it can affect someone other than the willing person, similar to what it creates in the soul of the willing person himself, either without condition or restriction, or with some conditions.

From what has passed, several matters become clear: **First:** that the criterion in this effect is the realization of certain knowledge (العلم الجازم) on the part of the practitioner of the supernatural act. As for the correspondence of this knowledge to external reality, it is not necessary, as was believed by the practitioners of the subjugation of planets (تسخير الكواكب) regarding the spirits (الأرواح) attached to the celestial bodies. It is possible that the angels and devils, whose names the practitioners of invocations and incantations extract and call upon through specific methods they possess, are of this category. Likewise, what the practitioners of spirit summoning (إحضار الارواح) believe regarding the presence of the spirit: they have no evidence for more than its presence in their imagination or senses rather than in the external world; otherwise, everyone present with them would see it, as everyone possesses a natural sense. By this, another ambiguity is resolved regarding the summoning of the spirit of one who is alive and awake, occupied with his affairs without being aware of it—for a single human has only one spirit. By this, another ambiguity is also resolved: that the spirit is a non-material substance (جوهر مجرد) that has no relation to a specific time and place over another. By this, a third ambiguity is also resolved: that a single spirit may appear to one person in a form different from the form in which it appears to another. By this, a fourth ambiguity is also resolved: that spirits may lie during summoning in their reports, and some may contradict others. The answer to all of this is that the spirit only appears in the perceptions of the person summoning it, not outside of them in the way we perceive natural material things.

**Second:** that the possessor of this effective will may rely in his will upon the power of his own soul and the stability of his "I-ness" (إنيته), as is the case with most practitioners of spiritual disciplines in their will; thus, it will inevitably be limited in power and restricted in effect for the willer and in the external world. Or he may rely therein upon his Lord, like the prophets and the friends of Allah (الأولياء) among the practitioners of servitude (العبودية) to Allah and the masters of certainty (اليقين) in Allah. They do not will anything except for their Lord and by their Lord. This is a pure will in which the soul from which this will emerges has no independence whatsoever, nor is it colored by any of the colors of psychological inclinations, nor does it have any reliance except upon the Truth (الحق). Thus, it is a divine will (ارادة ربانية), neither limited nor restricted.

The second category: if it acts in the context of challenge (التحدي), as is the case with most of what is narrated from the prophets, it is called a miracle (آية معجزة) — *an evidentiary sign that incapacitates others from producing its like*. If it is realized outside the context of challenge, it is called a charism (كرامة) — *a divine favor granted to a saint* — or the answering of a prayer (استجابة دعوة) if it was with a supplication. The first category: if it is through seeking information and aid from a jinn or spirit or the like, it is called soothsaying (كهانة); and if it is through an invocation, incantation, charm, or the like, it is called sorcery (سحراً).

**Third:** that since the matter revolves around the will in its strength, and it is of various levels of strength and weakness, it is possible for some to nullify the effect of others, such as the confrontation between sorcery and a miracle. Or some souls may not affect others if they differ in levels of strength, which is witnessed in the acts of hypnotism and summoning. A portion of the discussion on this will follow.

## (Scientific Discussion)

The sciences researching wondrous effects are many, and a universal statement on their classification and regulation is very difficult. The most well-known among their practitioners are what we mention: Among them is **Simiya (السيمياء)**: which is the science researching the mixing of volitional powers with specific material powers to obtain wondrous disposal in natural affairs. Part of it is the disposal in the imagination called "eye-sorcery" (سحر العيون), and this art is among the truest instances of sorcery. Among them is **Limiya (الليمياء)**: which is the science researching the manner of volitional effects through their connection with the strong, high spirits, such as the spirits entrusted with the planets and events and others, by subjugating them, or by connection and seeking aid from the jinn by subjugating them; this is the art of subjugations (التسخيرات). Among them is **Himiya (الهيمياء)**: which is the science researching the combination of the powers of the celestial world with the terrestrial elements to obtain wondrous effects; this is talismans (الطلسمات). For the celestial planets and heavenly positions have connections with material events, just as the elements, compounds, and their natural qualities do. So if the heavenly forms suitable for an event—such as the death of so-and-one, the life of so-and-one, or the survival of so-and-one, for example—are combined with the suitable material form, that results in obtaining the objective; this is the meaning of a talisman. Among them is **Rimiya (الريمياء)**: which is the science researching the employment of material powers to obtain their effects such that it appears to the senses that they are supernatural effects in some way; this is sleight of hand (الشعبذة). These four arts, along with a fifth art that follows them, which is **Kimiya (الكيميا)** — *alchemy* — researching the manner of changing the forms of elements into one another, were called among them "the five hidden sciences." Our teacher al-Baha'i said: "The best of the composed books in these arts is a book I saw in the city of Herat called *Kulla Sirr* (كله سر); its name was composed from the initials of the names of these sciences: *al-Kimiya*, *al-Limiya*, *al-Himiya*, *al-Simiya*, and *al-Rimiya*." End of the summary of his words.

Among the authoritative books therein are the summary of the books of Balinas, the epistles of al-Khusrawshahi, *al-Dhakhirah al-Iskandariyyah*, *al-Sirr al-Maktum* by al-Razi, *al-Taskhirat* by al-Sakaki, and *A'mal al-Kawakib al-Sab'ah* by the sage Tamtam al-Hindi.

Among the sciences attached to what has passed is the **science of numbers and magic squares (علم الاعداد والأوفاق)**: which researches the connections of numbers and letters for objectives, and the placement of the number or letters suitable for the objective in triangular, square, or other tables according to a specific order. Among them is **al-Khafiyah (الخافية)**: which is the breaking down of the letters of the objective or what suits the objective of the names, and the extraction of the names of the angels or devils entrusted with the objective, and the invocation with the incantations composed of them to attain the objective. Among the authoritative books therein according to them are the books of Sheikh Abu al-Abbas al-Tuni, Sayyid Husayn al-Akhlati, and others.

Among the arts attached to them circulating today are **hypnotism (التنويم المغناطيسي)** and **spirit summoning (احضار الارواح)**. They are, as passed, from the effect of the will and the disposal in the imagination. Many books and epistles have been authored regarding them, and the fame of their matter suffices from referring to them here. The purpose of what we have mentioned at length is to clarify the application of what applies of them to sorcery or soothsaying.
